fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 62 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the gate incorrectly allows the event instead of blocking it.

constraints.intent: When recent event count equals the limit, the condition should be true (gate should close), but with > it is false.
Checks that when recent count meets or exceeds limit the comparison used in the branch will correctly trigger blocking.
In the else branch (allowed path), recent count must be strictly less than limit; if it equals limit the else branch should not be taken.
When allowed is True, remaining must be non-negative (catches off-by-one leaks at the boundary).
